reasoning: The election is scheduled for June 3, 2025. In South Korean snap elections, public officials must typically resign 30 days prior to the election to run, which would be May 4, 2025 (aligning with the question's May 5 deadline). Although there is a strong push from civic leaders and pro-Yoon factions to draft Acting President Han Duck-soo, he did NOT register for the PPP primary by the April 15 deadline. Running now would mean either attempting an independent bid (historically unviable) or the PPP forcing a controversial draft that bypasses 11 registered candidates. Furthermore, the precedent set by Hwang Kyo-ahn in 2017—who also faced immense conservative pressure to run but chose to maintain neutrality and stability as acting president—strongly implies Han will ultimately decline. His April 22 comment of 'no decision yet' leaves the door slightly ajar, but structural factors and party logistics make it highly unlikely he will formally announce a run.
